L1.area: Template rendering and output formatting utilities

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where the template rendering engine is configured to disable HTML escaping by default, allowing user-controlled data inserted into templates to inject arbitrary scripts into rendered output.

L3.area: Mustache template compilation and rendering utilities in the core utility module
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the Mustache template compilation logic where HTML escaping is explicitly disabled. When user-controlled data is inserted into Mustache templates, it is rendered without sanitization, enabling script injection.

ground_truth.reason: The Mustache template compilation utility explicitly disables HTML escaping (escapeHTML(false)), allowing user-controlled content in templates to inject malicious scripts into rendered output.
